steps.title: Identify the key signal
steps.content: The phrase “no amount of justification... is likely to persuade” indicates these drivers have a fixed objection that no argument can overcome.

steps.title: Match the meaning to the choices
steps.content:
Exorbitant (unreasonably high) explains this perfectly: if drivers already believe the current toll is excessive, they would reject any justification for raising it further.

Warranted means justified (opposite of the problem), equivocal refers to ambiguity about the amount, and contentious means disputed—none capture that the toll is already too much.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
